Name: latent_space_exploration
Title: Exploring Latent Space for Adaptive Model Inference in Latency-Constrained Environments
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that leveraging latent space exploration can enhance model adaptation in real-time inference scenarios, allowing models to select relevant subspaces dynamically, thereby reducing inference latency without compromising accuracy.
Related Work: Existing methods for model adaptation often rely on fixed architectures or training regimes that do not account for dynamic input variations. For instance, HiLoRA utilizes hierarchical routing but still requires pre-defined LoRA modules. Our approach distinguishes itself by proposing an on-the-fly exploration of latent spaces that enables models to adaptively select relevant features based on current input, enhancing efficiency in latency-sensitive applications.
Abstract: In this proposal, we investigate the potential of latent space exploration as a mechanism for adaptive model inference in latency-constrained environments. Traditional approaches often struggle with the balance between model complexity and inference speed, particularly in real-time applications. We propose a novel framework that dynamically explores the latent space of pretrained models to identify and activate only the most relevant subspaces for given input data. This method aims to minimize computational overhead while maintaining robust performance. By employing Jacobian-based layer-skipping techniques, we will implement a mechanism that quantifies the importance of various latent dimensions and selectively activates them during inference. The proposed framework will be evaluated against standard models on various benchmark datasets, measuring throughput and accuracy to establish a Pareto frontier for latency-accuracy trade-offs. Our results are expected to demonstrate significant improvements in inference efficiency, paving the way for future research on dynamic model adaptation in real-world applications.
Experiments: 1. Implement a baseline model and the proposed latent space exploration framework on benchmark datasets (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet).
2. Quantify the importance of latent dimensions using Jacobian-based importance scores during inference.
3. Measure latency and accuracy as we vary the number of activated dimensions, establishing throughput-accuracy trade-offs.
4. Conduct ablation studies to isolate the effects of layer-skipping techniques and latent space selection on overall performance.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the complexity of accurately quantifying latent space importance, which may lead to suboptimal performance if not properly managed. Additionally, the proposed method may encounter limitations in extreme out-of-distribution scenarios where the latent space structure significantly deviates from training distributions.
